attempts.raw:
Based on my review of the 10 supplied papers, none provides evidence linking PLAGL2 to TCR inhibition or a related inhibitory T cell phenotype. The papers split into two disjoint groups: PLAGL2 studies confined to non-immune cancer/epithelial biology, and TCR/T-cell studies that never mention PLAGL2.

  "query": "PLAGL2 TCR inhibition",

There is no overlap connecting the regulator to the phenotype.",

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human, non-immune (ovarian cancer cell lines ES-2/SKOV3 and mouse xenograft)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: PLAGL2 mediates IGF2BP3 knockdown effect on ovarian cancer proliferation through mRNA stability regulation.
paper_assessments.rationale: PLAGL2 studied only as an oncogenic proliferation driver in ovarian cancer; no T cell or TCR content.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR-based therapies improved with T cells expressing a costimulatory chimeric antigen receptor (CoCAR).
paper_assessments.rationale: Costimulatory enhancement of TCR therapy; no PLAGL2 and no inhibitory phenotype addressed.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human, non-immune (PDAC tissue and cell lines)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: High PLAGL2 expression promotes proliferation, migration, invasion and EMT in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.
paper_assessments.rationale: PLAGL2 characterized as an EMT-promoting prognostic biomarker in PDAC; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Inducible TCR expression reduced surface TCRs and hampered function, with mispaired TCR dimers detected.
paper_assessments.rationale: Addresses TCR mispairing in dual-TCR cells; no PLAGL2 and no inhibitory signalling regulation.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Stress-induced epinephrine activates the PLAGL2-USP10 signaling loop to enhance HCC progression.
paper_assessments.rationale: PLAGL2 studied in stress-driven liver cancer progression; no T cell or TCR context.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: High TCR signal strength leads to T cell dysfunction, while low strength causes inertness.
paper_assessments.rationale: TCR signal strength and dysfunction studied, but PLAGL2 is never mentioned or tested.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human, non-immune (bladder cancer cell lines UMUC3/5637)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: MFSD12 exerts a tumor-promoting effect on bladder cancer, under the modulation of PLAGL2.
paper_assessments.rationale: PLAGL2 acts as transcription factor driving bladder cancer progression; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Synthetic TCR targeting PD-L1 enhances T-cell cytotoxicity against solid tumors.
paper_assessments.rationale: Engineered anti-PD-L1 TCR platform; no PLAGL2 and no PLAGL2-driven inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: rat, non-immune (newborn alveolar type II epithelial cells)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Upregulating PLAGL2 rescued hyperoxia-induced ferroptosis and apoptosis in alveolar epithelial cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: PLAGL2 studied in lung epithelial injury via HIF-1alpha/VEGF; no T cell or TCR content.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: PD-1 engagement's inhibitory effects on T cell signaling vary with TCR ligand potency.
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies PD-1 inhibition of TCR signalling, but PLAGL2 is never involved or examined.

parsed.tier_rationale: No retrieved primary study links PLAGL2 to TCR inhibition or a related inhibitory T cell phenotype. All PLAGL2 papers concern non-immune cancer/epithelial proliferation, EMT or survival; the TCR/T-cell inhibition papers (checkpoint, signal-strength dysfunction, engineered TCRs) never mention or perturb PLAGL2. There is no overlap connecting the regulator to the phenotype.
